rationale: Confirmed both Bead 2 targets are covered without redundancy: the new `.claude/workflows/**` claim (this bead) matches Bead 2's install target `.claude/workflows/ms-panel.js`; the existing `plugins/mindspec/**` claim (already in OWNERSHIP.yaml, untouched by this diff) covers Bead 2's authored source `plugins/mindspec/workflows/ms-panel.js` — no overlapping/redundant glob was added. Ordering is correct: plan.md explicitly states Bead 2 `depends_on: [1]` and 'the `.claude/workflows/**` claim must be visible at this bead's diffed ref before `.claude/workflows/ms-panel.js` is added' (plan.md:386-388), matching the ROOT-before-edit sequencing this bead implements. Walked Bead 2's full step list (plan.md:381-811): every other touched path (`internal/setup/**`, `plugins/mindspec/embed.go`, doc-sync at `.mindspec/domains/workflow/interfaces.md`) is already covered by pre-existing claims/doc-prefix rules — nothing Bead 2 needs is missing from this bead's scope. Ran `TestWorkflowOwnsClaudeWorkflows`: PASS, empirically confirming `attributeDomain` resolves `.claude/workflows/ms-panel.js` to `workflow`. This bead fully unblocks 9cyu.2 on the ownership axis.
findings: Claim non-redundancy verified: `.claude/workflows/**` (new) vs `plugins/mindspec/**` (pre-existing) are disjoint globs, each covering exactly one of Bead 2's two tracked-artifact copies.

Ordering verified textually: plan.md:386-388 states Bead 2 depends_on [1] specifically because the claim must be visible before the `.claude/workflows/ms-panel.js` edit lands — matches this bead's ROOT positioning.

No missing claims for Bead 2: internal/setup/** and plugins/mindspec/** (embed + install code) are both pre-existing entries in the workflow domain's OWNERSHIP.yaml paths list, unaffected by this diff.
